provider: HKUST 2025/26 BEng in Mechanical Engineering Major Requirements
note: This supplement is cohort-specific to students admitted in 2025-26 and binds only the plain BEng in Mechanical Engineering Major. It records all 55 unique explicit course codes in the intake-specific Major Requirements PDF, including every Engineering Fundamental alternative, Major Required alternative, and the four optional Option lists. Students may graduate without an Option; if an Option is selected, all of its requirements must be completed in addition to the Major Requirements. Each of the Energy, Engineering Design and Materials Options requires 3 courses and 9 credits from its specified list, and courses used as Major Required Courses or as Elective Courses of another MECH Option may not be counted again. The Research Option requires MECH 4995 for 6 credits. Courses appearing in more than one role are stored once with all memberships retained. All 55 explicit rows have fixed official credits; variable-credit MECH catalog courses outside the published requirement and Option lists are not added. The department's June 2026 study pattern for the 2025/26 cohort explicitly schedules MECH 2300 in Year 2 Spring, but the intake-specific Major Requirements PDF does not list MECH 2300 and instead publishes a MATH 2111, MATH 2350 or MATH 2351 alternative; MECH 2300 is therefore recorded only as an unresolved official-source conflict and is not claimed as a closed Major requirement here. MATH 1006 and communication/Common Core rows shown only in the pathway are likewise outside this Major-local coded list. Recommended Year and semester values are assigned only where the pathway identifies a listed code or a fully explicit alternative set; multi-term professional-development requirements retain their schedule in descriptions rather than being forced into one term. Anonymous MECH Option or Free Elective placements are not projected onto individual Option courses. The pathway states that an optional Major Option or any HKUST-offered Free Elective courses may help meet the 120-credit minimum; this open completion pool is not converted into a permanent closed graduation list. University Common Core, English, Legal Education and additional free electives remain outside this supplement.
